session_label: Ottoman Chunk 3 / Avenue 6: Sufi tekke holdings + dedicated Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi homonym audit
scope: Probe Konya Mevlana Müzesi / Yusufağa, Hacı Bektaş Veli Müzesi, Bursa İl Halk Kütüphanesi, and Edirne Selimiye Yazma Eserler for Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu / Hakîkî witnesses. Concurrent dedicated Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi (d. 893 AH / 1487 CE) homonym audit; the PRIMARY false-positive surface for Cihanşah Hakîkî searches in Ottoman tekke catalogues.
context_note: Chunk 3 of 4 splitting the lost Subagent C Ottoman scope (Subagent C terminated API-overloaded after 61 tool uses without writing output). Focus is the disambiguation work; expected witness count is 0 new Cihanşah hits. The deliverable IS the homonym audit.

method_summary.tier_1_portal_recon: WebSearch site-restricted + free-text WebSearch to enumerate published catalogue surfaces and TEIS / TDV İslâm Ansiklopedisi authoritative entries.
method_summary.tier_2_authoritative_canon_check: WebFetch TEIS HAKÎKÎ, Cihânşâh + TEIS HAKÎKÎ, Yûsuf-ı Hakîkî Baba entries + TDV Yusuf Hakîkî entry to enumerate the complete Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi manuscript surface for direct comparison against any Cihanşah candidates.
method_summary.tier_3_per_hit_disambiguation: Every 'Hakîkî' surface classified via date filter (pre/post-872 AH), context cluster (Persian-Turkic bilingual + Qaraqoyunlu titulature vs Bursa/Aksaray/Bektaşi Turkish-only), and Hurufi-adjacency analysis per v1.0 register methodology (Konya Hacı Bektaş BY0000010729 entry).

portals.method: WebSearch site-restricted + free-text + WebFetch TEIS / TDV authoritative entries

queries_executed.outcome: Zero indexed Cihanşah hits on konyamuze.gov.tr

TEIS Cihanşah entry + Şühûd-nâme Risâlesi article. Neither references a Yusufağa Kütüphanesi witness. TEIS confirms the canonical 5-witness Cihanşah corpus (BL Or 9493, Matenadaran 965, Süleymaniye Fatih 3808, Ankara DTCF İsmail Saib I.2221, Tehran University 8198); NONE in Konya.

queries_executed.query: 'Mevlana Müzesi' yazma katalog Hakîkî OR Cihanşah Karakoyunlu
queries_executed.outcome: Mevlana Müzesi has the Gölpınarlı 1311-volume catalogue (3 vols. published 1967-1972). One result is a Cihanşah Hakîkî Divan PDF (Muhsin Macit 2002 edition), but the edition uses the 4 first-known witnesses, NOT a Mevlana Müzesi codex.
Mevlana Müzesi 2430 is consistently attested as Yusuf Hakîkî Baba's Hakîkî-nâme/Divan in TEIS and TDV ansiklopedisi entries.

portals.verdict: PROBED_NEGATIVE_FOR_CIHANSAH; Mevlana Müzesi 2430 verified as Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi (NOT Cihanşah). Yusufağa Kütüphanesi shows zero indexed Cihanşah hits.
portals.yusuf_hakiki_finding: Mevlana Müzesi Kütüphanesi 2430 = Yusuf Hakîkî Baba's Hakîkî-nâme/Divan. Sadi Somuncuoğlu collection. Per TEIS + TDV. NOT Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu.

queries_executed.query: 'Hacı Bektaş Veli Müzesi' yazma 'Hakîkî' OR 'Cihanşah' OR 'Yusuf Hakîkî'
queries_executed.outcome: All hits are museum-info / tourism / general info pages. Key catalogue note: 'Books related to Bektashism were catalogued and sent to the National Library'; i.e. the Hacı Bektaş tekke manuscript corpus is now physically AT Milli Kütüphane Ankara, not at the museum-on-site. This means the v1.0 register entry konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729 (recovered via YEK) represents the surviving surface, and no new Hacı Bektaş witnesses are reachable via direct museum probing.

portals.verdict: PROBED_NEGATIVE; Hacı Bektaş tekke holdings absorbed into Milli Kütüphane; v1.0 register's konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729 entry already exhausts this surface.
portals.v1_register_overlap: Existing v1.0 entry konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729 (yek_id 709791): 150-folio Bektaşi mecmua with 'Hakîkî' at position 163 in 330-poet alphabetical-ish list with non-Hurufi local neighbors (Mestî/Bidarî/Resmî/Hevaî/Naimî/Riyazî). Marked 'candidate_probably_non_cihansah'; likely Anatolian aşık-tradition Hakîkî per the cross-cluster heuristic.

portals.portal_name: Bursa İl Halk Kütüphanesi / Bursa Yazma ve Eski Basma Eserler Kütüphanesi (İnebey)

queries_executed.query: site:bursa.kultur.gov.tr OR 'Bursa İl Halk' yazma 'Cihanşah' OR 'Yusuf Hakîkî'
queries_executed.outcome: Zero indexed Cihanşah / Yusuf Hakîkî hits on Bursa cultural-ministry domain.

queries_executed.query: 'Bursa İl Halk Kütüphanesi' OR 'Bursa Yazma' Hakîkî Divan Karakoyunlu
queries_executed.outcome: Bursa Yazma ve Eski Basma Eserler Kütüphanesi confirmed as Turkey's 3rd-largest manuscript library (8,389 manuscripts) housed in 14th-century İnebey medrese; ALL search results are library-info pages or general Bursa-cultural-heritage articles, none containing Hakîkî/Cihanşah/Karakoyunlu witness references. Notably the canonical 5 Cihanşah witnesses (BL/Matenadaran/Süleymaniye/Ankara/Tehran) are repeatedly enumerated WITHOUT a Bursa entry.

portals.verdict: PROBED_NEGATIVE; Bursa İl Halk / İnebey holdings do not surface a Cihanşah witness in indexed search. Caveat: site-restricted Google indexing may miss JS-rendered catalogue records.
portals.yusuf_hakiki_homonym_concern: Bursa is THE primary surface for Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi attribution risk (he taught at Bursa). The probe finding 0 Yusuf Hakîkî hits in indexed search suggests Yusuf Hakîkî's manuscripts are NOT primarily held in Bursa; TEIS / TDV attestation puts them at Konya (Mevlana Müzesi 2430, İl Halk Uzluk 6968), Manisa (Murâdiye 1296), Süleymaniye (Hacı Mahmud Efendi 2974, 2574), and possibly Cairo Hidiviyye. Bursa as a Yusuf Hakîkî FP-surface is OVER-INDEXED in the original Avenue 6 hypothesis.

queries_executed.outcome: Zero indexed Cihanşah hits on edirne.gov.tr domain.

queries_executed.query: 'Edirne' OR 'Selimiye' yazma eserler Hakîkî Yusuf Cihansah
queries_executed.outcome: Selimiye Yazma Eser Kütüphanesi confirmed as a YEK child portal hosting 2,600 manuscript volumes (3,384 items) + 5,617 printed works. All results are library-overview / institutional articles; none reference a Cihanşah / Hakîkî witness.

portals.verdict: PROBED_NEGATIVE; Edirne Selimiye holdings do not surface a Cihanşah witness in indexed search. The single confirmed Cihanşah text reference on TEIS does not cite Selimiye holdings.

homonym_disambiguation_audit.purpose: Per-hit classification of every 'Hakîkî' surface encountered in this chunk. The audit IS the deliverable.

classification_categories.cihansah_qaraqoyunlu: Date pre-872 AH (1467 CE), Persian-Turkic bilingual content, Qaraqoyunlu titulature, Hurufi-cluster mecmûʿa adjacency
classification_categories.yusuf_hakiki_bursavi: Date post-893 AH (1487 CE) with Bursa/Aksaray/Bektaşi context, Turkish-only content, 'Hakîkî Baba' / 'Hakîkî Bursavi' titulature, Bektashi-Hurufi şathiyat content
classification_categories.anatolian_asik_hakiki: 17th-19th c. folk tradition, non-Hurufi local neighbor poets in mecmûʿa

hits.source: TEIS HAKÎKÎ, Yûsuf-ı Hakîkî Baba entry + TDV İslâm Ansiklopedisi Yusuf Hakîkî entry
hits.collection: Sadi Somuncuoğlu collection (acquired into Mevlana Müzesi)
hits.reasoning: TEIS + TDV both attest this codex as Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi's Divan, NOT Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu. Yusuf Hakîkî d. 893 AH / 1487 CE in Aksaray; he is Somuncu Baba's son and a Bayrami-Melami / Bektashi-Hurufi adept. The codex content matches his Turkish-only mystical-didactic register, distinct from Cihanşah's Persian-Turkic bilingual royal-court ghazals.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: VERY HIGH; a casual catalogue search for 'Hakîkî' in Konya Mevlana Müzesi would surface this entry as a 5-witness Cihanşah corpus candidate. Disambiguation requires reading the codex content or consulting TEIS/TDV.

hits.reasoning: Mahabbet-nâme is uniquely Yusuf Hakîkî's mystical mesnevî, not a Cihanşah work. Cihanşah's corpus is Divan-only (ghazals + tuyuğs). A Mahabbet-nâme attribution to 'Hakîkî' is diagnostically Yusuf Hakîkî.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: LOW (work-title disambiguates) but the author-name index 'Hakîkî' alone could surface this as a false positive in unstructured search.

hits.work: Mahabbet-nâme (175 folios, 11 lines/page, copied by Muhammed ibni Paşa in 894 AH / 1489 CE; one year after author's death)
hits.reasoning: Same as Uzluk 6968; Mahabbet-nâme is uniquely Yusuf Hakîkî's. The 894 AH (1489) copy-date is one year post Yusuf Hakîkî's death (893 AH); the colophon explicitly identifies him as the recently-deceased author.
Mystical-theological prose treatise attributed to Yusuf Hakîkî in TEIS. Distinct from Cihanşah's Divan-only corpus.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: LOW (prose treatise; Cihanşah has no attested prose works).

hits.reasoning: Mystical-theological treatise attributed to Yusuf Hakîkî in TEIS/TDV. Per TDV the codex also contains three sufism-related sub-treatises 'likely attributable to Yûsuf Hakîkî'. Distinct from Cihanşah's Divan-only corpus.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: LOW (prose treatise + theological-doctrinal register; Cihanşah has no attested prose works).

hits.shelfmark: Sadi Somuncuoğlu private collection (two copies of Hakîkî-nâme/Divan); Mevlana Müzesi 2430 was one of these
hits.reasoning: Family-collector pedigree (Somuncuoğlu = descendants of Somuncu Baba, Yusuf Hakîkî's father). Provenance alone identifies the work as Yusuf Hakîkî's.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: MODERATE; provenance is not catalogued in every aggregator; a generic 'Hakîkî Divan' index entry could be confused with Cihanşah's Divan.

hits.source: TEIS (provenance unverified; 'Cairo Hidiviyye Kütüphanesi, undetermined content and volume')
hits.shelfmark: Cairo Hidiviyye Kütüphanesi (likely modern Dār al-Kutub al-Miṣriyya)
hits.work: Et-Tesnîm + Er-Rahîk el-Mahtûm (both attributed but not located in indexed catalogues)
hits.reasoning: TEIS-attributed Yusuf Hakîkî works in Cairo Hidiviyye but no folio count / shelfmark / inspection. Could be a different Hakîkî or a misattribution. Flag for v2.0 follow-up via Dār al-Kutub.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: LOW (work titles disambiguate; Cihanşah corpus is Divan-only).

hits.source: v1.0 register konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729 (re-verified for this chunk)
hits.collection: Konya Hacı Bektaş-ı Veli Dergahı (via YEK)
hits.work: 150-folio Bektaşi mecmua with 'Hakîkî' at position 163 in 330-poet alphabetical-ish list
hits.reasoning: Mecmûʿa adjacency analysis: Hakîkî sits with non-Hurufi local neighbors (Mestî/Bidarî/Resmî/Hevaî/Naimî/Riyazî). Nesîmî at position 38, Şah Hatâî at 345, Şah İsmail Bahadır at 321; far elsewhere in the codex. The diagnostic Hurufi-doctrine cluster adjacency is NOT codex-layout-confirmed. Likely an Anatolian aşık-tradition Hakîkî per the Afyon Gedik 13918 cluster heuristic.
hits.fp_risk_for_cihansah_search: HIGH (already documented in v1.0 as a discovered FP).

audit_summary.notable_finding: Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi's manuscript surface is concentrated in Konya (Mevlana Müzesi 2430 + İl Halk Uzluk 6968), Manisa (Murâdiye 1296), and Istanbul (Süleymaniye Hacı Mahmud Efendi 2974 + 2574). Bursa; the city where Yusuf Hakîkî taught; is NOT a primary holding location for his manuscripts. The 'Bursa = Yusuf Hakîkî FP-surface' hypothesis in the original Avenue 6 scope is OVER-INDEXED. The real FP-surface is Konya Mevlana Müzesi 2430.
audit_summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new Cihanşah witnesses. The dedicated Yusuf Hakîkî audit confirms the FP-surface is well-characterized; 6 attested Yusuf Hakîkî witnesses (Mevlana Müzesi 2430, İl Halk Uzluk 6968, Manisa Murâdiye 1296, Süleymaniye Hacı Mahmud Efendi 2974 + 2574, Sadi Somuncuoğlu private) plus 1 indeterminate (Cairo Hidiviyye).

summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new Cihanşah Qaraqoyunlu witnesses added. The Yusuf Hakîkî homonym audit is the substantive deliverable; it enumerates the Yusuf Hakîkî Bursavi manuscript surface (6 attested + 1 indeterminate) and pinpoints Konya Mevlana Müzesi 2430 as the highest-risk FP surface for casual 'Hakîkî Divan' searches.
methodological_findings: TEIS (teis.yesevi.edu.tr) and TDV İslâm Ansiklopedisi (islamansiklopedisi.org.tr) are the authoritative disambiguation surfaces for Cihanşah vs. Yusuf Hakîkî homonyms in Turkish-language scholarship. Direct portal probing of Konya/Bursa/Edirne local domains adds nothing beyond what TEIS/TDV already enumerate.

Bursa is NOT a primary FP-surface for Yusuf Hakîkî manuscripts despite being his teaching location. The real concentration is Konya (Mevlana Müzesi + İl Halk Uzluk) + Manisa Murâdiye + Süleymaniye Hacı Mahmud Efendi.
Hacı Bektaş tekke holdings have been absorbed into Milli Kütüphane Ankara; the v1.0 entry konya_haci_bektas_by0000010729 (recovered via YEK) already exhausts the on-site surface.

Edirne Selimiye holdings (2,600 manuscript volumes) show zero indexed Cihanşah or Yusuf Hakîkî hits; consistent with Yusuf Hakîkî's geographical concentration in Konya/Aksaray/Manisa and Cihanşah's being collected in non-Ottoman channels (BL, Matenadaran, Tehran).
residual_gaps_for_v2: Cairo Hidiviyye Kütüphanesi (modern Dār al-Kutub al-Miṣriyya): confirm whether Yusuf Hakîkî's Et-Tesnîm + Er-Rahîk el-Mahtûm are present and distinguish from any potential Cihanşah witness via direct catalogue probe.
Konya Mevlana Müzesi 2430 + İl Halk Uzluk 6968: direct codex inspection would confirm Yusuf Hakîkî attribution and rule out any chance of mixed-codex Cihanşah excerpt embedded inside.

Bursa İnebey Yazma Kütüphanesi (8,389 manuscripts, separate from İl Halk): not directly probed; recommend Playwright-headed pass on its dedicated catalogue if v2.0 expands to Anatolian provincial Bektaşi-Hurufi mecmua-mining.
